response.justification: Candidate brings valuable geospatial domain expertise and strong distributed systems background, but lacks current C++ proficiency—the role's primary requirement. Most recent C++ work was 10+ years ago; current focus is TypeScript/Node.js. Geospatial experience (GISCollective, Green Map) is GIS-focused rather than routing/navigation-specific. Learning agility is evident, but ramping from TypeScript to production C++ routing pipelines carries risk.
strengths: Direct geospatial domain expertise: built and maintains GISCollective platform and web GIS applications for Green Map
Strong distributed systems background: microservices infrastructure experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second with performance optimization
Proven ability to work across technical stack: successfully picked up multiple languages (DLang, Java, Swift, C#) and domains (infrastructure, embedded, web, mobile)
concerns: C++ expertise gap: last production C++ work was 10+ years ago at National Instruments; recent work entirely in TypeScript/Node.js, creating ramp-up risk for primary language requirement
Routing specificity mismatch: GIS experience is data collection and visualization (GISCollective, Green Map) rather than routing algorithms, traffic data, or navigation tile generation
No Rust alternative: zero Rust experience despite it being listed as acceptable substitute, limiting fallback options

key_factors.factor: Distributed systems and performance optimization experience
